title: centaurea nigra subsp. nemoralis
intro: Centaurea nigra subsp. nemoralis, also known as the woodland knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Centaurea nigra subsp. nemoralis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
